channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan Q1 business investment stalls amid Iran war concerns
item.description: Japanese corporate capital expenditure paused after a year of strong growth as Middle East conflict uncertainty weighs on business confidence, with spending remaining flat in Q1.

item.title: 🇯🇵🇨🇳 Japan rejects Chinese accusations of new militarism
item.description: Defense Minister Shinjiro Koizumi pushed back against Chinas criticism of Japans defense buildup, saying Tokyo is not pursuing militarism while Beijing rapidly arms itself.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇵🇭 China conducts patrols near Scarborough Shoal after Philippines warning
item.description: Chinas military and coast guard carried out patrols near the disputed South China Sea feature, a day after Manila said it remained under threat from Beijing despite recent easing in US-China tensions.

item.title: 🇰🇷🇯🇵 South Korea and Japan discuss military logistics support deal
item.description: Seoul confirmed ongoing discussions with Tokyo about a potential military logistics support agreement as both US allies deepen defense cooperation amid regional security concerns.

item.title: 🇸🇾🇺🇸 Syrias Sharaa holds phone call with Trump, presidency says
item.description: Syrias interim leader Ahmed al-Sharaa spoke by phone with President Donald Trump, according to the Syrian presidency, in a rare direct contact between the two countries.

item.title: 🇲🇩 Moldova Orthodox Church Patriarch Resigns Amid Geopolitical Divide
item.description: The patriarch of one of Moldova's two competing Orthodox churches resigned, a split that exposes the country's divisions between pro-European and pro-Russian orientations. A successor is to be appointed later this week.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Child Killed in Ukrainian Drone Strike on Russian-Held Kherson
item.description: A Russian-appointed official reported a Ukrainian drone struck an apartment building in the Russian-held part of Kherson region, killing a child and injuring 11 people.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Businesses Remain Downbeat on Economic Outlook
item.description: British businesses remain pessimistic about the economic outlook despite recent government efforts, according to CBI and Institute of Directors surveys. Smaller firms showed somewhat less negative sentiment.

item.title: 🌏 Asia's AI Chip Boom Could Spark Regional Economic Renaissance
item.description: Investors are piling into South Korea and Taiwan for semiconductor stocks, but the AI boom is powering a broader economic resurgence across North Asia through stronger household consumption, rising investment and widening tax coffers.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Netanyahu appoints Arrow-3 innovator Shmuel Ben Ezra as national security adviser
item.description: Ben Ezra, a key figure in developing Israel's Arrow 3 missile defense system, takes over the crucial role left vacant since Tzachi Hanegbi's ouster in October 2025.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇵🇭 US Coast Guard conducts first joint patrol with Philippines near Scarborough Shoal
item.description: The patrol, amid Manila's concerns over potential Chinese construction, marked the first time a US Coast Guard vessel joined Philippine forces for maritime cooperative activities in the contested South China Sea hotspot.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China's crackdown on offshore brokerages closes popular route to overseas markets
item.description: Beijing's toughest restrictions yet on offshore trading platforms are ending a popular avenue for mainland investors, with analysts saying the move goes beyond investor protection to broader capital control objectives.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Seeks Changes to US-Iran Ceasefire Deal
item.description: President Trump has requested amendments to the proposed US-Iran ceasefire agreement, including modifications related to the Strait of Hormuz and removal of highly enriched uranium. The deal includes a 60-day cessation of violence and potential sanctions relief for Iran.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan Defense Minister Criticizes China Military Buildup
item.description: Japan Defense Minister Shinjiro Koizumi rejected Beijing claims of "new militarism" and criticized China for its "huge arsenal" and lack of transparency. The exchange occurred at the Shangri-la Dialogue defense summit in Singapore.

item.title: 🇧🇾🇺🇦 Belarus Emerges as Potential New Launchpad for Russian Offensive Against Ukraine
item.description: Ukraine is bolstering defenses near Belarus amid warnings that Lukashenko could allow Russian forces to use Belarusian territory for a new offensive. Belarus hosts Russian nuclear weapons and has integrated its defense industry with Moscow's war machine.

item.title: 🇩🇪 German FM voices serious concern over Israels advance into southern Lebanon
item.description: Germanys foreign minister said Israels push further into southern Lebanon is cause for serious concern, as fighting expands beyond the Litani River.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran reopens most entrances to 18 underground missile sites struck in war
item.description: Satellite imagery shows Iran has restored 50 out of 69 tunnel openings at underground missile facilities targeted by US-Israeli strikes, with most missiles surviving the attacks.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇪🇺 US Castigates Europe Over Defense Spending at Singapore Summit
item.description: The US criticized NATO allies at the Shangri-La Dialogue over insufficient defense spending, while Western European officials insisted the alliance remains resilient amid growing global threats.

item.title: 🇺🇸⚔️ US Military Personnel Being Targeted Using Location Data, Pentagon Letter Shows
item.description: US forces deployed to war zones have been targeted using commercially available location data, according to reports fielded by military officials, highlighting vulnerabilities in data privacy.

item.title: 🇰🇷🇯🇵 South Korea and Japan Discuss Military Logistics Support Agreement
item.description: South Korea and Japan discussed a potential military-logistics support agreement at the Shangri-La Dialogue, though Seoul remains cautious about the politically sensitive pact.

item.title: 🌏🇨🇳🇺🇸 Indo-Pacific Nations Hedge Between Rising China and Distracted US
item.description: Countries in the Indo-Pacific are strengthening defense ties as they navigate between China's rapid military rise and an increasingly distracted United States, according to a Reuters analysis from Singapore.

item.title: 🇸🇦🇶🇦💰 Saudi Shares Gain, Qatar Slips on Iran-US Deal Uncertainty
item.description: Saudi Arabia's stock exchange closed higher while Qatar's slipped as markets struggled to find direction amid uncertainty over a possible ceasefire extension in the Middle East.

item.title: 🇸🇾 Syrias Sharaa Holds Phone Call With Trump
item.description: Syrias de facto leader Ahmad al-Sharaa held a phone call with US President Donald Trump to discuss sanctions relief and Syrias reconstruction, marking a significant diplomatic engagement between the new Syrian government and the United States.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US-Israel Military Cooperation Bill Faces Bipartisan Pushback
item.description: US lawmakers from both parties are pushing to repeal a provision in the National Defense Authorization Act that would deepen integration between US and Israeli militaries, citing concerns about oversight and entanglement in regional conflicts.

item.title: 🇿🇦 South Africa postpones World Cup travel due to visa crisis
item.description: South Africa national team delayed their departure to Mexico just 11 days before the World Cup opener against the hosts due to visa issues affecting players and officials.
